no: Use Spellhold-Studios/Rogue-Rebalancing (https://github.com/Spellhold-Studios/Rogue-Rebalancing). Both SHS and TotoR115/rr report VERSION ~v4.92~, but TotoR115's lib/ds.tpa (md5 d93e05d0...cde038) ships the old `LAF detectable_spells END` signature, which corrupts STATS.IDS on modern engines. SHS's ds.tpa (md5 a0d8fa1d...82b954) uses the modern DS library API (detectable_spells_setup + per-table calls with STR_VAR path) and leaves STATS.IDS intact. mleduque/rogue_rebalancing mirrors SHS.

conflicts.reason: Functionality provided by Tweaks and Tricks (per II install notes)

M&G #280 vs RR #1: both rewrite Assassin/Bounty Hunter CLABs and strip SPCL332 backstab — hard conflict for these components. M&G #400 vs RR #4: both overhaul all bard CLABs — hard conflict. Other component combos are safe. Install RR first if using non-conflicting components.
conflicts.myComps: #1 Thief Kit Revisions, #4 Bard Kit Revisions
conflicts.source: Both modify clabth02/03.2da and clabba01-04.2da with incompatible ability systems, 2026-03

conflicts.reason: Safe — different aspects. ET cn:300 (Pickpocketing) modifies store steal-difficulty flags and creature scripts, not thief skill 2DAs. ET cn:400 (Detect Illusions) appends new CLAB rows to thief kits — safe after RR cn:1 which modifies existing rows. ET cn:600 (Use Poison) uses EEex extended skill system (stat 690) and appends CLAB rows — independent of RR's Assassin poison rework. Install RR before ET. No shared file conflicts for selected components.
conflicts.theirComps: #300 Epic Pickpocketing #400 Epic Detect Illusions #600 Use Poison
conflicts.source: ET appends CLAB rows (safe); RR modifies existing rows; ET cn:300 uses store files; RR cn:3 uses SKILLRAC.2DA; no cross-detection, 2026-04

conflicts.reason: Component-level analysis: (1) RR cn:0 (Dual-wielding, WEAPPROF row 32) is nullified by ToF cn:81100 which externalizes proficiencies to Lua — do not use both. (2) RR cn:2 (Thief HLA revisions: Acid Trap, Danger Sense in LUTH0-4.2DA) is overwritten by ToF cn:60200 (Revised HLAs) — do not use both. (3) RR cn:1 (Kit Revisions) has soft overlap with ToF cn:40470 on SPCL414.SPL — different aspects (RR changes mechanics, ToF changes description). Install RR before ToF. (4) RR cn:3 (Racial Skills, SKILLRAC.2DA) has no ToF overlap — safe. (5) ToF cn:40450 (Evasion via CLAB splstate) is a separate system from RR's HLA evasion — they coexist.
conflicts.myComps: #0 Dual-wielding (conflicts cn:81100) #1 Kit Revisions (install order) #2 Thief HLAs (conflicts cn:60200) #3 Racial Skills (safe)
conflicts.theirComps: #40450 Thief Evasion #40470 Rebalanced traps #60200 Revised HLAs #81100 Revised Proficiency
conflicts.source: ToF cn:81100 wipes WEAPPROF to 99s+Lua; ToF cn:60200 overwrites LUTH0-4.2DA; RR cn:0/2 removed

spells: Proper spell progression tables for bards

sum: Overhauls thief and bard kits with 12 rebalanced versions. Reworks sneak attack, trap-setting, and bard song to make rogues more tactically interesting.

0.desc: Proper dual-wielding implementation for thieves and bards

ki.description: Rogue Rebalancing v4.92 ships an outdated ds.tpa (Detectable Spells) library that corrupts STATS.IDS by adding duplicate nonsense entries. This causes WeiDU to fail when any later mod (especially EET_End) tries to DECOMPILE_AND_PATCH scripts containing CheckStat() lookups. Identified by argent77 (April 2026). Three users independently confirmed the issue.
ki.workaround: Use Angel's Rogue Rebalancing fork (which has an updated ds.tpa) instead of the original v4.92. The fork resolves the STATS.IDS corruption.
